title: Scotland–Spain–Portugal–Italy Travel Boost as Ryanair Expands at Glasgow Prestwick Airport for Summer 2026

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Ryanair launches Summer 2026 at Glasgow Prestwick with 10 routes to Spain, Portugal, and Italy, boosting Scotland’s Mediterranean connectivity. Scotland-Spain-Portugal-Italy Travel Boost as Ryanair Expands at Glasgow Prestwick Airport for Summer 2026 - Travel And Tour World Home » Airlines News of Italy » Scotland-Spain-Portugal-Italy Travel Boost as Ryanair Expands at Glasgow Prestwick Airport for Summer 2026 A significant boost to leisure connectivity between Scotland and key destinations in Spain, Portugal, and Italy has been announced with the launch of the Ryanair Summer 2026 programme at Glasgow Prestwick Airport . The expanded schedule has been designed to strengthen links between western Scotland and some of Europe's most sought-after sunshine and cultural hotspots. With ten routes confirmed and increased frequencies on several high-demand services, travellers from Scotland are being offered broader choice, improved flexibility, and sustained low-fare access to the Mediterranean region.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The development is being supported by two aircraft based at Glasgow Prestwick, reinforcing operational reliability during the busy summer period. Popular destinations across Spain's mainland and islands, Portugal's Algarve region, and Italy's Tuscany region are being prioritised to match sustained outbound demand. The move is being viewed as a strong vote of confidence in both the airport and the regional travel market. As bookings open for Summer 2026, passengers across Ayrshire and the wider West of Scotland catchment area are being presented with expanded opportunities for holidays and short city breaks across Southern Europe. The Summer 2026 schedule from Glasgow Prestwick Airport has been structured around ten European routes, combining established leisure destinations with popular short-break cities. The network is being positioned to cater primarily to outbound holiday demand from Scotland to Spain, Portugal, and Italy, markets that continue to perform strongly during peak travel months.
